prose:
Computed atom-condensed Fukui indices for formaldehyde.

Method provenance
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic), f⁰ (radical) and dual descriptor; identification of most electrophilic and most nucleophilic sites.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density-fitting; exact four-center integrals) in PySCF v?.
Geometry provenance: single-point Fukui analysis on the user-supplied Cartesian geometry in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz (no further optimisation).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (singlet); cation (N – 1) state: charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion (N + 1) state: charge –1, multiplicity 2.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`) → PySCF backend.
Convergence & checks: SCFs for N, N±1 states converged (integrity.status = "ok").
Warnings (verbatim from engine):
• fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• schema_header: common header present
Integrity verdict: trustworthy = true
